Selected work

Real newsrooms. Real numbers. Real deadlines.

NDTV Convergence · Case Study
Building a regional newsroom's digital voice from scratch
Aug 2023 – Sept 2024

Joined as Sub Editor & Social Media Executive to launch two brand-new regional verticals — NDTV Rajasthan and NDTV MPCG — with zero existing audience. Led all day-to-day digital operations, curating and publishing approximately 80% of all content across every platform. Also supported NDTV India during major national events under team leads.

Major events covered
2024 General Elections Assembly Elections Budget 2024 Olympics & Paralympics Cricket World Cups US Elections Ram Mandir Inauguration Samarth by Hyundai Banega Swasth India · Dettol
Written journalism — published under my byline

Beyond social media management, I wrote and edited articles published directly on NDTV's regional news websites — covering politics, governance, elections, civic issues, and national events. These carry my byline and are publicly verifiable. Topics covered include state elections, budget analysis, political developments across Rajasthan, MP and Chhattisgarh, and national stories.

Read my NDTV Rajasthan articles → Read my NDTV MPCG articles →

Real-time social coverage of the US Presidential Debate — breaking news graphics, fact-checks, live blogs, and explainer carousels, all conceptualised, designed, and published by me.

NDTV India · Budget 2024 Campaign
50+ graphics in a single day — Budget 2024 content production
On Union Budget day 2024, I was the primary graphic designer for NDTV India's social media coverage. I independently conceptualised, researched, and designed over 50 graphics — published live across NDTV India's handles as the Budget was being presented. I also created the master Canva templates that the wider team used throughout the day, enabling the entire team's visual output. Every data point personally verified, every design built from scratch under live deadline pressure.
50+
Graphics published in one day
10–15
Data & sector cards — fully solo
+Team
Templates I built, team used
Live
Real-time, under deadline
What this actually means: Budget day is one of the most intense news events of the year — dozens of announcements in rapid succession, each needing to be understood, verified, designed, and published before the news cycle moves on. Producing 50+ graphics under those conditions, solo, is not a design task. It's editorial judgement, data literacy, and production speed working simultaneously.
Sample live posts — verify the work

Independent Project · 2023 – 2024
Indian Elections 2024 — an independent election coverage account
While covering elections professionally at NDTV, I also built and ran an independent Instagram account dedicated to tracking Indian elections — from five state elections to the 2024 General Elections. No newsroom behind it. No editorial team. Just one journalist who wanted to do more, communicating complex electoral data, candidate profiles, and live results to a public audience in a clear, accessible format.

Brand content campaigns for Jockey, P&G, and the NBA
Created and executed social media content for major brand campaigns. Led the content team and was recognised as Top Performer (Delhi) for both the Jockey and NBA campaigns.
Top performer — Delhi (Jockey & NBA) Content strategy Copywriting
2017 – 2018
Parliamentary Communications · Lok Sabha
Research, speechwriting and press communications for a sitting MP
Worked directly with a sitting Lok Sabha MP on a range of political and parliamentary communication tasks — independently and without public attribution. Maintained strict neutrality and confidentiality throughout.
What I produced
Parliamentary speeches Budget session press notes Policy research briefs Research articles Constituent communications Legislative content
This work required the ability to research complex policy quickly, write in a formal legislative register, and produce content that could be delivered on the floor of Parliament and in press briefings. Work covered Budget sessions and broader policy areas. The MP's name and party are withheld to maintain political neutrality.
Policy writing Legislative research Political communications Mid 2024 – 2025
Independent Project · Ministry of AYUSH collaboration
International Yoga Day — event coordination and digital certificate system
Served as Chief Coordinator for a community-level International Yoga Day event in Lucknow, organised in collaboration with the Ministry of AYUSH, Lucknow University, and Patanjali Yogpeeth. Beyond the event itself, independently designed and deployed a full digital certificate generation system for participants — built from scratch with no formal technical training.
The digital system I built
Built a self-contained HTML-based certificate generator with a QR-code entry flow, Google Forms background logging, and canvas-based name overlay. Resolved mobile compatibility issues and Devanagari text rendering. Hosted on Netlify. Used by 200+ participants on the day of the event.
200+ users on launch day Self-taught HTML / Netlify Ministry of AYUSH Event coordination QR code system Certificate generation Google Forms integration

Writing

Where the historian in me meets the journalist. Updated regularly on Medium.

This section updates as I write. Follow medium.com/@namita.ipad1 for the latest — the articles below are a selection, not the full archive.
Medium · June 2026
The AI Newsroom Has Arrived. What Happens to Journalism Now?
A sharp analysis of how artificial intelligence is reshaping Indian newsrooms — the opportunities, the risks, and the question nobody in the industry wants to answer honestly. Written from the inside, by someone who has worked in a national newsroom and holds an IIM Bangalore certification in AI in digital media.
IIM Bangalore AI certified Media analysis Future of journalism

2026
Medium · Feb 2026
Reading India's Budget Like a Household Ledger
What does Budget 2026 look like when you stop reading numbers and start thinking about everyday lives? This piece translates complex fiscal policy into the language of real households — the kind of explainer journalism that makes public interest stories actually public.
Policy explainer Public interest journalism Budget 2026
Read on Medium →
Feb 2026
Medium · Jan 2026
When Equality Exists Only on Paper
India calls itself the world's largest democracy. But what happens when constitutional rights and lived reality diverge? A sharp, historically grounded piece on the gap between the promise of equality and its practice — drawing on two master's degrees worth of context.
Democracy & rights Political commentary Modern India
Read on Medium →
Jan 2026

Jan Jagriti — a newspaper built from scratch

7 editions. Mastered InDesign on the job. Full newsroom, zero shortcuts.

IIMC · Hindi Journalism Department
Jan Jagriti — a 4-page Hindi newspaper, produced end to end

As part of IIMC's practical journalism training, our cohort produced Jan Jagriti — a full Hindi newspaper across 7 editions. Every issue was written, edited, fact-checked, and laid out entirely by students. I taught myself Adobe InDesign during this process and became the primary layout designer for the team — while also training classmates to use it. Across editions I rotated through every role: reporter, sub-editor, layout designer, and editor.

7
Editions published
4
Pages per edition, fully designed
Mastered
Adobe InDesign — zero formal training
Roles across editions
Primary layout designer Mastered InDesign — under live deadline Reporter Sub-editor Editor Taught teammates InDesign Hindi writing & editing
Stories I wrote and editions I designed
New Parliament Building inauguration Budget 2023 analysis Wrestlers' protest (WFI) Manipur violence Republic Day coverage World Environment Day Climate change & farming Delhi CM residence controversy Dara Shikoh's tomb discovery Same-sex marriage debate Bharat Jodo Yatra Messi's World Cup win

"IIMC introduced me to InDesign — but I mastered it entirely on my own, under real deadlines, for a real publication. By the end I was the person others came to when they needed help. Every layout you see in these editions was built by me learning in real time."

— Namita Lohani

Video journalism

From NDTV India's national channel to solo-produced IIMC packages.

NDTV India · YouTube Short
27 December का दिन क्यों है इतिहास के पन्नों में दर्ज
A short-form history explainer produced and published on the main NDTV India YouTube channel — one of India's largest Hindi news platforms. Conceptualised, scripted, and produced during my time at NDTV Convergence. The piece uses the "on this day in history" format to make historical context accessible and shareable for digital audiences.
Published on NDTV India — national channel YouTube Shorts History explainer Digital content
Student journalism — independently produced at IIMC
IIMC · Video journalism
Nepal legalises same-sex marriage — a ground report
A solo-produced news package on Nepal becoming the first South Asian country to legalise same-sex marriage — researched, scripted, edited, and produced entirely independently. International human rights journalism, student-made from the ground up.
Solo produced Scriptwriting Video editing International affairs
IIMC · Video journalism
Delhi floods — government response and ground reality
A solo-produced news report on the Delhi floods and the government's relief measures — independently scripted, shot, and edited, with a piece-to-camera closing segment. Demonstrates on-screen reporting confidence alongside full production capability.
Solo produced Piece to camera Ground reporting Civic journalism

About me

The longer version — for people who actually read.

I'm a journalist, digital strategist, and self-taught creative based in Lucknow. My career has taken me from IIMC's newsroom training to NDTV's live election coverage — and the through-line has always been the same: find the story, then find the best way to tell it.

I have two master's degrees — one in Journalism & Mass Communication, one in Modern History — and a habit of teaching myself whatever tool a job requires. Adobe Premiere Pro, Tableau, AI integrations — no classroom, just curiosity and deadlines.

I speak Hindi, English, Sanskrit, and enough German to get myself in trouble. I write on Medium, play chess, and believe the best stories are always one layer deeper than the obvious one.

Namita Lohani
Journalist · Digital Strategist
